channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: La Agencia Espacial Europea lanza sus propios agentes de IA generativa
item.description: La Agencia Espacial Europea (ESA) da un paso adelante en su estrategia de inteligencia artificial (IA).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition
item.description: Learn how OSINT is shaping the future of intelligence and security. The post OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Lupin Season 4: Omar Sy Hints at a Different Return Format
item.description: Lupin, the popular Netflix series, might be returning but in a different way, according to star Omar Sy.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: Why HR professionals struggle with big data
item.description: Over the next 15 years, more than 12 million people will retire, while technological progress will lead to major changes in occupations. According to the World Economic Forum, almost half of the skills required of employees today will change in the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Zoom wants you to drop Microsoft Office and Google Workspace for its new AI software tools
item.description: Zoom adds even more AI-powered features to make sure you don’t just think of it as ‘that’ video calling app.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Last of Us season 2's release date has been set for mid-April, and I'm certain that I know when its next trailer will arrive
item.description: HBO has confirmed that its hugely popular dystopian drama series will return in less than two months.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Legacy Software Hindering AI Adoption at Many Organizations
item.description: Outdated software applications are creating roadblocks to AI adoption at many organizations, with limited data retention capabilities a central culprit, IT experts say.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The iPhone 16E is here. Here are 5 ways it’s different from the iPhone SE
item.description: Apple has introduced its first new product of 2025: the iPhone 16E. The new iPhone replaces the iPhone SE from Apple’s lineup—the company’s entry-level, budget iPhone.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Musk’s plan to replace government workers with AI could create chaos
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk’s plan to cut $2 trillion in US government spending apparently includes replacing tens of thousands of federal workers with AI, but the move could produce disastrous results, several AI experts say.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft updates VS Code C# Dev Kit extension
item.description: Microsoft announced updates to its C# Dev Kit for Visual Studio Code extension, including enhancements to Razor/Blazor Hot Reload and for debugging and testing Blazor web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Everything new on Hulu in March 2025 – including Sean Baker's Oscar-nominated drama Anora
item.description: Anora is just one of the new movies coming to Hulu in March 2025. Here's the full list of the titles coming to the platform next month.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: iPhones may soon be able to use satellite connections indoors
item.description: Sending messages via satellite on iPhones should soon be as easy to as using your mobile network signal, as Apple and satellite partner Globalstar put a new satellite constellation into space.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New iPhone 16e renews Apple’s push into budget smartphone market
item.description: Apple has faced increased competition from Google and Samsung, in the budget smartphone market and in the AI space
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Why CX is every brand’s biggest opportunity
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: John Sabino, Fast Company

item.title: National Science Foundation cuts 10 percent of staff
item.description: Nearly 170 employees at the National Science Foundation (NSF) who were still on probation were fired by the Trump administration on Tuesday, union officials told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: AI-created disinformation could bring down banks
item.description: A new report by UK analyst firm Say No to Disinfo and communications firm Fenimore Harper indicates a high risk that AI-generated disinformation could create bank runs that could bring down financial institutions, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sea turtles return to the Atlantic Ocean off Florida after being stunned by the cold
item.description: Eight green sea turtles stunned by the cold during a rare snowfall in Florida have been released back into the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bannon: Musk 'wants to impose his freak experiments' on US
item.description: Former White House chief adviser turned conservative commentator Steve Bannon blasted billionaire Elon Musk over his efforts to reduce the size of the federal government and gain more power in the U.S. "Musk is a parasitic illegal immigrant," he said during an interview with the website UnHerd.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Want to transform networking? Empower the missing users
item.description: As 2025 kicked off, I wrote a column about the network vendor landscape – specifically, which networking players will step up and put their efforts into finding new applications with solid business benefits that could enable a network transformation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mistral turns focus toward regional LLMs with Saba release
item.description: French AI startup Mistral is turning its focus toward providing large language models (LLMs) that understand regional languages and their parlance as a result of rising demand among its enterprise customers. “Making AI ubiquitous requires addressing every culture and language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Key strategies for MLops success in 2025
item.description: Integrating and managing artificial intelligence and machine learning effectively within business operations has become a top priority for businesses looking to stay competitive in an ever evolving landscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How to set up a home network like a pro
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you ensure a smooth online experience with a reliable, secure home network setup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to keep AI hallucinations out of your code
item.description: It turns out androids do dream, and their dreams are often strange. In the early days of generative AI, we got human hands with eight fingers and recipes for making pizza sauce from glue. Now, developers working with AI-assisted coding tools are also finding AI hallucinations in their code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
